context: This chapter describes the shraddha ceremony performed for all ancestors (pitrs). It details the proper rituals of tarpana (water offerings), the importance of feeding brahmins, the auspicious times for performing shraddha, and the immense merit gained by those who honor their departed forefathers with devotion and prescribed rites.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
पितॄणां श्राद्धविधिं ब्रूहि केशव।
येन कृतेन तृप्यन्ति पितरः पितामहाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Pitṝṇāṁ śrāddha-vidhiṁ brūhi Keśava,
yena kṛtena tṛpyanti pitaraḥ pitāmahāḥ.
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Keshava, tell me the procedure of the shraddha ceremony for the ancestors, by performing which the fathers and grandfathers become satisfied.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि श्राद्धकल्पं सनातनम्।
यत्कृत्वा मानवाः सर्वे पितॄणां तृप्तिकारकाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi śrāddha-kalpaṁ sanātanam,
yat kṛtvā mānavāḥ sarve pitṝṇāṁ tṛptikārakāḥ.
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarshkya, I shall describe the eternal ordinance of the shraddha ceremony, by performing which all human beings bring satisfaction to their ancestors.

verses.hindi:
अमावास्यां तथा पर्वे पितॄणां दिवसे शुभे।
श्राद्धं कुर्यात् प्रयत्नेन द्विजान् सम्पूज्य भक्तितः॥
verses.transliteration:
Amāvāsyāṁ tathā parve pitṝṇāṁ divase śubhe,
śrāddhaṁ kuryāt prayatnena dvijān sampūjya bhaktitaḥ.
verses.translation: On the new moon day and on auspicious ancestral occasions, one should perform shraddha with great effort, worshipping the brahmins with devotion.

verses.hindi:
कुशैः पवित्रं कृत्वा तु शुचौ देशे समाहितः।
पितॄन् आवाह्य विधिवत् तर्पणं परिकल्पयेत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Kuśaiḥ pavitraṁ kṛtvā tu śucau deśe samāhitaḥ,
pitṝn āvāhya vidhivat tarpaṇaṁ parikalpayed.
verses.translation: Having purified oneself with kusha grass, in a clean place, with a concentrated mind, one should invoke the ancestors according to the prescribed rites and offer tarpana (water oblations).

verses.hindi:
तिलमिश्रं जलं दद्यात् पितृभ्यो दक्षिणामुखः।
तृप्यतां पितरः सर्वे इति मन्त्रं समुच्चरन्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tilamiśraṁ jalaṁ dadyāt pitṛbhyo dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ,
tṛpyatāṁ pitaraḥ sarve iti mantraṁ samuccaran.
verses.translation: Facing south, one should offer water mixed with sesame seeds to the ancestors, reciting the mantra: 'May all the ancestors be satisfied.'

verses.hindi:
पिता पितामहश्चैव तथैव प्रपितामहः।
माता पितामही चैव प्रपितामह्यथापि च॥
verses.transliteration:
Pitā pitāmahaścaiva tathaiva prapitāmahaḥ,
mātā pitāmahī caiva prapitāmahyathāpi ca.
verses.translation: The father, the grandfather, and the great-grandfather; the mother, the grandmother, and the great-grandmother — all these are to be honored in the shraddha.

verses.hindi:
एतेषां नामगोत्रेण तर्पणं परिकल्पयेत्।
जलेन तिलमिश्रेण प्रीयन्तां पितरः सदा॥
verses.transliteration:
Eteṣāṁ nāma-gotreṇa tarpaṇaṁ parikalpayed,
jalena tilamiśreṇa prīyantāṁ pitaraḥ sadā.
verses.translation: One should offer tarpana mentioning their names and gotras (lineages). May the ancestors ever be pleased by the water mixed with sesame.

verses.hindi:
ब्राह्मणान् भोजयेत् पश्चाद् यथाशक्ति विचक्षणः।
यत्नेन पाकशुद्धेन हविष्यान्नेन शोभनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Brāhmaṇān bhojayed paścād yathāśakti vicakṣaṇaḥ,
yatnena pākaśuddhena haviṣyānnena śobhanam.
verses.translation: Afterwards, the wise person should feed brahmins according to his means, with carefully prepared, pure sacrificial food.

verses.hindi:
एकोऽपि भोजितो विप्रः श्राद्धे यः श्रद्धयान्वितः।
पितॄणामक्षयां तृप्तिं प्रददाति नरोत्तमः॥
verses.transliteration:
Eko'pi bhojito vipraḥ śrāddhe yaḥ śraddhayānvitaḥ,
pitṝṇām akṣayāṁ tṛptiṁ pradadāti narottamaḥ.
verses.translation: Even a single brahmin, fed with faith during the shraddha ceremony, bestows imperishable satisfaction upon the ancestors. Such is the noblest of men.

verses.hindi:
अन्नं वस्त्रं सुवर्णं च गां भूमिं तिलसर्षपान्।
दद्याद् ब्राह्मणमुख्येभ्यः पितॄणां प्रीतिकारणात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Annaṁ vastraṁ suvarṇaṁ ca gāṁ bhūmiṁ tilasarṣapān,
dadyād brāhmaṇamukhyebhyaḥ pitṝṇāṁ prītikāraṇāt.
verses.translation: Food, clothes, gold, a cow, land, sesame, and mustard seeds — these should be given to the chief brahmins for the sake of pleasing the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
श्राद्धे दत्तं हुतं जप्तं यत्किञ्चित् क्रियते नरैः।
तत्सर्वमक्षयं विद्यात् पितॄणां तृप्तिकारणम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrāddhe dattaṁ hutaṁ japtaṁ yat kiñcit kriyate naraiḥ,
tat sarvam akṣayaṁ vidyāt pitṝṇāṁ tṛptikāraṇam.
verses.translation: Whatever is given, offered in fire, or chanted by men during the shraddha — know all of that to be imperishable and the cause of satisfaction of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
गयायां पिण्डदानेन तथा वाराणसीपुरे।
प्रयागे चैव तीर्थेषु श्राद्धं कोटिगुणं भवेत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Gayāyāṁ piṇḍadānena tathā Vārāṇasīpure,
Prayāge caiva tīrtheṣu śrāddhaṁ koṭiguṇaṁ bhavet.
verses.translation: By offering pinda (rice balls) at Gaya, and similarly at Varanasi and Prayaga and other holy places, the merit of the shraddha becomes multiplied ten-million-fold.

verses.hindi:
मधु मांसं च पायसं खीरं चैव घृतान्वितम्।
पितॄणां प्रीतिजनकं श्राद्धे दद्यात् प्रयत्नतः॥
verses.transliteration:
Madhu māṁsaṁ ca pāyasaṁ khīraṁ caiva ghṛtānvitam,
pitṝṇāṁ prītijakanaṁ śrāddhe dadyāt prayatnataḥ.
verses.translation: Honey, offering-food, rice pudding, and milk-rice enriched with ghee — these bring delight to the ancestors and should be offered with care during the shraddha.

verses.hindi:
आश्विने कृष्णपक्षे तु पितृपक्ष इति स्मृतः।
तत्र श्राद्धं प्रकुर्वीत सर्वपितृप्रतर्पणम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Āśvine kṛṣṇapakṣe tu pitṛpakṣa iti smṛtaḥ,
tatra śrāddhaṁ prakurvīta sarvapitṛpratarpaṇam.
verses.translation: The dark fortnight of the month of Ashvina is known as Pitru Paksha (the fortnight of the ancestors). During that period one should perform the shraddha for the satisfaction of all ancestors.

verses.hindi:
संवत्सरे व्यतीते तु श्राद्धं नित्यं प्रकल्पयेत्।
मासि मास्यथवा कुर्यात् पक्षे पक्षे च तत्परः॥
verses.transliteration:
Saṁvatsare vyatīte tu śrāddhaṁ nityaṁ prakalpayed,
māsi māsy athavā kuryāt pakṣe pakṣe ca tatparaḥ.
verses.translation: After a year has passed, one should regularly perform the shraddha. Alternatively, one may perform it every month, or every fortnight, with diligence.

verses.hindi:
श्राद्धकाले तु यो मोहाद् अकृत्वा पितृतर्पणम्।
भुङ्क्ते स पितृशापेन नरकं प्रतिपद्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrāddhakāle tu yo mohād akṛtvā pitṛtarpaṇam,
bhuṅkte sa pitṛśāpena narakaṁ pratipadyate.
verses.translation: He who, out of delusion, eats without performing the tarpana for the ancestors at the time of shraddha, falls into hell by the curse of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
शय्या आसनदानं च छत्रोपानत्कमण्डलुम्।
दद्यात् श्राद्धे द्विजाग्र्येभ्यः पितॄणां प्रीतिवर्धनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Śayyā āsanadānaṁ ca chatropānat kamaṇḍalum,
dadyāt śrāddhe dvijāgryebhyaḥ pitṝṇāṁ prītivardhanam.
verses.translation: A bed, a seat, an umbrella, shoes, and a water-pot should be given to the best of brahmins during shraddha — this increases the delight of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
अपि स्वल्पं यदा दत्तं श्रद्धया परयान्वितम्।
तदानन्त्यफलं ज्ञेयं पितॄणां तोषकारणम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Api svalpaṁ yadā dattaṁ śraddhayā parayānvitam,
tad ānantyaphalaṁ jñeyaṁ pitṝṇāṁ toṣakāraṇam.
verses.translation: Even a small offering, when given with supreme faith, should be known to yield infinite results and is the cause of the ancestors' contentment.

verses.hindi:
पितरः प्रेतलोकस्थाः तृप्ताः श्राद्धेन भक्तितः।
आयुः प्रज्ञां श्रियं पुत्रान् ददति श्राद्धकारिणे॥
verses.transliteration:
Pitaraḥ pretalokasthāḥ tṛptāḥ śrāddhena bhaktitaḥ,
āyuḥ prajñāṁ śriyaṁ putrān dadati śrāddhakāriṇe.
verses.translation: The ancestors dwelling in the world of the departed, satisfied by the shraddha performed with devotion, bestow long life, wisdom, prosperity, and progeny upon the performer of the shraddha.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन श्राद्धं कुर्यात् विचक्षणः।
पितॄणां तृप्तिदं नित्यं पुत्रधर्मं समाचरन्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmāt sarvaprayatnena śrāddhaṁ kuryāt vicakṣaṇaḥ,
pitṝṇāṁ tṛptidaṁ nityaṁ putradharmaṁ samācaran.
verses.translation: Therefore, with every effort, the wise one should perform the shraddha, ever fulfilling the duty of a son by giving satisfaction to the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
इति श्रीगरुडपुराणे सारोद्धारे पितृश्राद्धवर्णनं
नाम त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः॥
verses.transliteration:
Iti Śrī Garuḍa Purāṇe Sāroddhāre Pitṛśrāddhavaraṇaṁ
nāma trayodaśo'dhyāyaḥ.
verses.translation: Thus ends the thirteenth chapter, entitled 'The Description of the Shraddha for All Ancestors,' in the Saroddhara of the Shri Garuda Purana.
